articles.title: Trump heads to China for state visit. And, how the war in Iran has affected inflation
articles.summary: Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.

articles.title: Kuwait says Iran attacked an island where China is helping to build a port
articles.summary: Kuwait accused Iran of launching a failed attack earlier this month on an island where China is helping build a port. And a U.S. ambassador revealed Israel sent its Iron Dome air-defense to the United

articles.title: As Trump goes to China, what do Americans say about tariffs, Iran and world standing?
articles.summary: Most Americans see China as one of the United States' biggest rivals or adversaries, but largely as an economic threat, according to a new Chicago Council/NPR/Ipsos poll.

articles.title: F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
articles.summary: The agency’s top food official will step in as acting commissioner, after Dr. Makary’s tumultuous run as the nation’s top food, drug, tobacco and medical device regulator.

articles.title: Pentagon Puts Iran War Cost at $29 Billion as Hegseth Deflects on Funding
articles.summary: The estimate from the Defense Department’s top budget official was $4 billion higher than two weeks ago. Secretary Pete Hegseth declined to say when the administration would request extra money for th

articles.title: Officials Raise Quality Concerns About Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Repairs
articles.summary: Work on the iconic National Mall basin appears behind schedule. President Trump initially said he had picked the contractor, then said he did not know it.

articles.title: Kuwait Accuses Iran of Trying to Infiltrate Its Territory
articles.summary: Kuwait’s interior ministry said a group of armed Iranians arrived at Bubiyan Island in the Persian Gulf on May 1 aboard a rented fishing boat and exchanged fire with Kuwaiti soldiers.

articles.title: The economic chilling effect of Trump's immigration crackdown
articles.summary: New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.

articles.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy filmed a reality show, funded by firms he regulates
articles.summary: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.

articles.title: More Cancer Patients Are Taking Ivermectin. Mel Gibson and Joe Rogan Might Be Why.
articles.summary: Prescriptions for antiparasitic drugs spiked after the actor claimed it cured his friends of cancer, a new study finds.

articles.title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
articles.summary: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.

articles.title: CPI Shows Inflation Accelerating to 3.8% Annually in April After Weeks of War in Iran
articles.summary: The Consumer Price Index rose 3.8% in April from a year earlier as higher energy costs replaced tariffs as the driver of higher prices for Americans.

articles.title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
articles.summary: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.

articles.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
articles.summary: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April.  Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.

articles.title: Trump rejects Iran's ceasefire proposal response. And, Congress to tackle ICE funding
articles.summary: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.

articles.title: The Iran War Is Taking the Color Out of Japan’s Best-Known Snack Bags
articles.summary: The food giant Calbee said shortages of naphtha, a crude-oil derivative used in inks, were forcing it to switch to black-and-white packaging for its salty products.

articles.title: 24 hours with 3 teenage birders: Welcome to the World Series of Birding
articles.summary: The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.

articles.title: Drive 5 hours or fly 20 minutes? Remote towns suffer from lack of year-round flights
articles.summary: A federal program that pays airlines to operate in small and rural communities could have its budget cut in half, leaving parts of the country with no flight options.

articles.title: Israel to hold military tribunal for Palestinians accused in 2023 Hamas-led attacks
articles.summary: Israel is planning a military tribunal to prosecute several hundred Palestinians accused of perpetrating the Hamas-led attacks of Oct. 7, 2023.

articles.title: Military drills in the Philippines affect the country's most vulnerable communities
articles.summary: Military drills disrupted fishing livelihoods in the Philippines, sparking protests as locals are caught between a great power rivalry and rising economic pressures linked to the Iran war.

articles.title: Israeli lawmakers set up tribunal, allow for death penalty for October 2023 attackers
articles.summary: The measure passed 93-0 in the 120-seat Knesset, or parliament, reflecting widespread support for punishing those found responsible for what was the deadliest attack in Israel's history.

articles.title: EU diplomats agree to sanction Hamas leaders and Israeli settlers
articles.summary: The unanimous decision by the European Union, agreed to on Monday, was sparked by growing outrage over the devastation in Gaza during the Israel-Hamas war.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
